target: Shinpei Ajiro

Born May 8, 2000 (18). O-type blood. Eldest son of underwater archaeologist Ajiro family; lost parents at 8. Fostered by Alain Kofune. Influenced by Alain and daughter Ushio, he discovered culinary passion. Moved to Tokyo after middle school. Attends culinary school for high school diploma. Specializes in chicken curry. Loves mysteries/sci-fi since childhood; favorite author Ryunosuke Nagumo. Takes steps back for objective, bird's-eye view. Afraid of water and cannot swim. [Summarized - see notes]
Appoke

A Wakayama dialect word meaning "idiot" or "dummy."
Rather than a harsh insult, it's a somewhat softer expression
that is often used playfully between close friends.
Amenonuhoko

A sacred treasure of Hitogashima Shrine, named after the mythological spear that shaped Japan. Believed to have existed for millennia, its drops formed Onogoro Island. Forged from rust-proof meteoric iron, ceremonial yet lethal. Handle remade over time; current oak version dates to Edo period. [Summarized - see notes]
Alain's Field

A private vegetable garden located on the way from Restaurant Kofune to Mt. Takanosu.
Tended to with a meticulous focus on the soil itself,
the fresh vegetables harvested here are one of the secrets to Kofune's flavor.
Event Flyer

A secret live concert featuring popular idol and Hitogashima Island native, Kaori Koyuba! There will also be a talk show and a handshake event! Locals and visitors alike are all welcome to join us!
Date/Time: July 24, 2018, starting at 20:00~ • Location: Special Stage on the Hitogashima Shrine Grounds • Sponsors: Hitogashima Chamber of Commerce • Hitogashima Shrine
* Participants for the handshake event will be selected by lottery.
Event Horizon

The observational boundary. It refers to a cliff-like concept that steadily closes in with each loop. It represents the limit of the scope where a do-over is possible on the timeline, creeping up behind Shinpei as time flows forward. The further away this cliff is, the more time he has to try again; the closer it gets, the less leeway he has left. If this cliff catches up to him and he falls, Shinpei's death will be finalized, and from then on, the timeline observed by Haine will become cemented as objective reality.
Ushio's Present ①

A present given to him by Ushio in a dream.
However, he doesn't know what it is yet.
Ushio's Present ②

14 years ago, the shock of eating Ryunosuke caused Haine to panic, and her persona as the human girl Hoine separated from Hiruko's body. The mud comprising the right eye she took then drifted in the sea for years, eventually copying Ushio in a chance encounter. After maturing and awakening as the Observer's right eye, it transcended time to be handed over to Shinpei. It marked the end of everything, and the beginning as well.
Ushio's Shadow ①

July 18th. A beach cleanup drive was held at Omoto Beach,
the coastline spreading out in front of Restaurant Kofune.
It was an event to collect trash in preparation for a local Hitogashima Island ritual.
While participating, Ushio and Mio spotted a young girl standing on the rocks.
Her appearance was purportedly a dead ringer for Ushio.
Ushio's Shadow ②

July 24th. The Summer Festival, Hitogashima Island's biggest event, was held. Catching a glimpse of a woman who looked like Ushio, Shinpei left Mio and the others behind and instinctively ran after her. Though he lost sight of her in the crowd, after making his way to Omoto Beach, Shinpei reunited with the Ushio who was supposed to be dead. Unable to recall her own death, nor even the reason why she was there, Ushio innocently confessed her feelings to Shinpei.
Ushio's Smartphone

A smartphone received from the police officer Totsumura.
He claimed Ushio handed it over to him the day before she died.
It's locked with a password, so its contents cannot be viewed.
Ushio's Necklace

Made from a seashell that Shinpei gifted her back in elementary school.
It seems she cherished it greatly, as it has been carefully maintained.
Ushio was apparently wearing it right up until the accident.
Ushio's Ability ①

The abilities Ushio gained access to after recovering her memories are as follows:

1. Copying: The ability to scan and copy a target.
2. Physical Prowess: The incredible physical capabilities possessed by Shadows.
3. Hair Blades: The ability to weaponize her hair into sharp blades.
4. Memory Sharing: The ability to let others share and relive her own memories.
Ushio's Ability ②

When Shinpei loops, all events that occurred in the previous cycle are undone. However, Ushio alone is an exception; by looping alongside Shinpei, she retains her memories from previous cycles. It has become clear that Ushio can carry over not just memories, but also data scanned in previous cycles. This could be used to bring along weapons obtained in previous loops.
Uozumi Inn

We are a traditional inn boasting a 140-year history since the Meiji era. Located just a five-minute walk from the Hitogashima Island port, our inn sits upon a quiet hill overlooking the Kii Channel. Every room offers a panoramic view of the sea, and the breathtaking sunset seen from our open-air baths is sure to become a lifelong memory. Please enjoy our seasonal fish dishes, changing with the seasons.
Erika-chan

Real name unknown. 21 years old. Blood type A.
Works part-time at Snack Miyako.
She loves music and has a bit of a geeky streak.
She is currently obsessed with 2.5D idols.
She receives passionate advances from Totsumura, but she doesn't seem entirely opposed to it.
Her snaggletooth is her charm point.
Trump Card

The trump card Hizuru had been hiding.
Taking advantage of the fact that Shide's mud (his armor material) contains zero information,
the plan is to have Ryunosuke possess that mud.
Ryunosuke will print his own data onto the stolen mud to acquire a new body,
while Shide, stripped of it, will lose his defenses.
Umi-okuri (Sea Send-off)

Hitogashima Island has a unique belief in castaway gods, stating that Hiruko-no-Mikoto resides within objects washed ashore and blesses them with bountiful catches. Umi-okuri (Sea Send-off) is a ritual held twice a year in summer and winter, where trash from the cleanup drive is carried on a shrine palanquin to the grounds, then burned to return it to the gods. It is said to bestow a blessing of good health upon the attendees.
Omoto Beach

A beautiful beach situated on the southern side of Hitogashima Island.
It is beloved as a swimming spot.
For those born and raised on the island, it is an extremely familiar location.
Restaurant Kofune is built right beside the road that runs along Omoto Beach.
"I'll Protect You"

When Shinpei and Ushio were children,
Ushio's prominent blonde hair and blue eyes made her a target for bullies.
Worried about her, Shinpei promised, "I'll protect you,"
and gave her a seashell for her birthday.
Ushio still cherishes that seashell to this day.
I Was Infected

The mysterious flash of light at Ushio's funeral.
That flash was emitted when the Shadow Shiori copied Shinpei.
Unbeknownst to him, Shinpei had been infected with Shadow Sickness since the very first loop.
Audio File

A mysterious audio file recorded on Hizuru Minakata's smartphone. Recorded in a Wakayama-accented female voice. Warns of impending disaster on Hitogashima Island involving Haine, culminating in mass casualties at Summer Festival 24th. Requires aid from Shinpei Ajiro. [Summarized - see notes]
Kaori Was Dragged Into It…

July 22nd, 9:00 AM.
While lost in thought on the beach, Shinpei witnesses Shadow Mio murder and copy Totsumura,
and is subsequently stabbed himself.
Furthermore, Kaori, who happened to be passing by, is captured by Mio.
Unable to process what was happening,
Shinpei's consciousness faded to black.
Kaori's Notes

Population of 700. • Famous for mugwort mochi and salted squid guts. • Some people bought mochi right after arriving at the port. Looks tasty! • The sight of houses lining the slopes is so nostalgic. • ⇒ The sea breeze feels so good, the hills aren't even tiring! • Hitobuchi-sensei is still teaching here! • ⇒ I'll go see her if I have time. • The policeman is energetic and fun, too! • ⇒ The crime rate is virtually zero. • You can only cross over to Torashima (Tiger Island) at low tide.
Kaori's Love Letter

Dear Ajiro-kun, Leaving Hitogashima Island for Tokyo next week. Heartbroken over leaving you behind. Thank you for books—read every one. Some sci-fi was tough but fascinating. The first book's futuristic love note inspired this letter. I'll send it to my future self; hope we read it together… Love, Kaori Koyuba [Summarized - see notes]
Shadow's Ability ①

Shadows appear to possess the ability to spawn new Shadows.
These born Shadows can apparently replicate not only the physical appearance
but also the clothing and memories of the person they copy.
Shadow's Ability ②

Shadows possess superhuman strength/speed. Their true body is a flat shadow; attacking their 3D form fails. Destroying the flat shadow kills them. They avoid touch on shadows, dodging steps. Weakness: Flat shadow must be struck for elimination. [Summarized - see notes]
Shadow's Ability ③

Even if a Shadow's three-dimensional body sustains damage, they can recover using incredible healing abilities. However, damage sustained by their flat true body cannot be healed. In such cases, the damage is projected onto their 3D form, seemingly accompanied by pain. Furthermore, these injuries appear impossible to treat with standard medical care such as disinfection or sutures.
Shadow's Ability ④

Shadow duplicates items by copying them. They can print their own forms or small objects onto strands of hair. Printing energy-based objects (e.g., power sources) only copies shape—no function. Function requires erasing original source. Benefits include reprinting depleted items, restoring full capacity without limits. [Summarized - see notes]
Shadow's Ability ⑤

Two copy methods exist: transformation (projecting scanned data) and parturition (body splitting). Haine's offspring reproduce once; their descendants cannot. Transformation is limitless for all generations, but originals must be erased within seven days or disintegrate. [Summarized - see notes]
Shadow Sickness

Something akin to an endemic disease passed down on Hitogashima Island.
It is said that if you contract Shadow Sickness, you will start seeing a shadow that looks exactly like you,
and those who see their shadow will be killed by it.
Many people used to fall ill with it in the past,
but it has rarely been heard of since the postwar era. It is said that those who fell ill were purified by Hiruko-sama.
Video of Shadow Mio

A video capturing the figure of Mio standing in front of Kofune at around 9:00 AM on July 22nd.
The Mio in the video stands holding a kitchen knife, staring up at the second floor for about five minutes,
before turning and walking off towards the mountains.
Because the real Mio was taking a bath at this time,
it is impossible for this to be the real her. It is precious footage proving the existence of Shadow Mio.
Interference with the Past

Shinpei's desperate feelings reached Ryunosuke's ears.
Just as Haine reached out from her memories to leave a handprint on the present Shinpei,
it might be possible for Shinpei to do something similar.
Gasoline

A type of petroleum product. Used in various applications as a liquid fuel.
There are different types depending on its use and manufacturing method.
It is highly volatile and burns violently even with an extremely small ignition source like static electricity,
so mishandling it runs the risk of a major accident.
Capgras syndrome

A delusion in which one misidentifies a friend, spouse, parent, or other close relative
as having been replaced by an identical-looking impostor.
It can sometimes be caused by dementia or a head injury.
Records remain of a mass outbreak of this on Hitogashima Island in the past.
Karikiri's Spare

Karikiri has survived for over 300 years by switching bodies. In case he died or suffered severe injuries from an accident or illness, he preserved his own past bodies, but they were destroyed in the war. However, there's a high probability that the body of his predecessor, Iwao Karikiri, who died after the war, is still preserved somewhere, and using it as a spare would explain why there were two Shides. Though how exactly he operated it remains unclear.
Masahito Karikiri ①

The chief priest of the Hitogashima Shrine. The head of the Karikiri family, who manages the island's rituals.
He is very talkative, and if left alone, he will keep chatting endlessly.
He also has a gamer side to him,
sometimes traveling to Tokyo purely for gaming events.
He apparently attended a university in Tokyo during his student days.
Masahito Karikiri ②

During the Edo period, Shidehiko Hishigata copulated with the god Haine and made her give birth. Perhaps due to the nature of Shadows, the child born grew to look exactly like Shidehiko. Seeing this, Shidehiko schemed to use Haine's power to transplant his memories into his son and rejuvenate himself. He has continued to make her give birth to him, surviving for 300 years— that is the true nature of Masahito Karikiri and Shide.
Curry Sandwich Recipe
developer_comments: カレーサンドのレシピ

target: Poke holes in a chicken breast with a fork, massage in salt and sugar, • sprinkle with sake, and microwave for 4-5 minutes. • Once cooked through, let it cool as is. • Shred the chicken and place it in a bowl. • Add mayonnaise and curry powder, mixing well. Taste and adjust. • Spread butter on sliced bread and top with frill lettuce. • Place the mixture from Step 2 on top to make a sandwich. • Cut in half and it's ready to serve.
developer_comments: カレーサンドのレシピ

target: The Observer's Power

Shinpei's right eye views time from a high-dimensional perspective, observing countless parallel worlds. The observed world becomes objective reality; achievements there vanish in his own. He cannot fully master this power without "death" as a trigger, causing loops upon dying. Haine has not stolen Shinpei's right eye. [Summarized - see notes]
Keychain

A keychain of the popular character "Egg Gentleman Eas-ta~kun".
Since the first wave of merchandise was released in the early 2000s,
the Egg Gentleman series has garnered support across a wide range of ages.
Kaori has loved this character since she was a child,
and Shinpei remembers her collecting Egg Gentleman goods
for things like pencil boards and pencil cases.
Deja Vu

The feeling that one has previously experienced a situation
that is in fact entirely new.
Sometimes associated with memories from a past life.
Spider Form

Just before 8:00 AM on July 22nd. The mud enveloping Shide absorbed nearby Shadows
and morphed into the shape of a giant spider.
With a wider field of vision and reach than his humanoid form,
Shinpei is tormented by movements unimaginably swift for a creature of that size.
The plan failed, and Shide's sharp arm impaled Shinpei.
Game Playboy

Monthly gaming mag with over 30-year history; price fixed at 290 yen since launch. Slogan: "Make games 100x better" – covers news, honest reviews, unique perspectives. Totsumura reads June issue featuring "Indie Games to Play Now" and "Gaming Salons in Asia-Pacific." Gravure spread highlights rising idol Kaori Koyuba. Koba Mart sells 2 copies monthly; they sell out instantly, so Hitogashima Island children are unaware. [Summarized - see notes]
The Sewers

There has always been a vast space running beneath Hitogashima Island.
Combined with countless underground passageways and sewers dug by the military,
it has morphed into an extremely complex maze.
No one knows its full layout anymore.
It is believed the Shadows utilized the sewers and such when moving around unseen.
Koba Mart

The only supermarket on Hitogashima Island.
Its predecessor was the Kobayakawa Store, which operated since before the war. Opens at 8:00 AM.
The owner is Tatsuo Kobayakawa.
His wife Asako, whom he married 10 years ago, is a licensed pharmacist,
allowing them to sell everything from daily necessities to medicines.
Koba Mart is inextricably linked to the daily lives of the islanders.
The Kobayakawa Family Disappearance

July 23rd, 8:00 AM.
Even though it was opening time, Koba Mart's shutters remained closed.
When the neighbor, Shiomi-san, grew suspicious and peeked into the main house, it was completely empty.
With no signs of having left the island by ferry,
the three members of the Kobayakawa family vanished into thin air overnight.
Shiori Kobayakawa ①

A third-grade elementary school student. The daughter of Tatsuo and Asako Kobayakawa, who run Hitogashima Island's only supermarket, "Koba Mart." She is a bit precocious and strong-willed. Her friends tease her because she is a poor swimmer. On July 21st, she began to drown in the sea, but her life was saved by Ushio's desperate rescue efforts. However, the shock of losing the Ushio who saved her left her unable to speak.
Shiori Kobayakawa ②

Shiori Kobayakawa was a Shadow.
Something flashed at Ushio's funeral venue during the 1st and 2nd loops,
but nothing flashed in the 3rd loop.
The reason for the difference was because Shinpei told her about Shadows,
putting her on guard.
The flash in the 1st and 2nd loops is believed to be from a copy taking place, but it is unknown who at the funeral was targeted.
Alain Kofune

Born in 1959, 59 years old. Originally from France.
When he was 32, he met and fell in love with Kotoko, then 20, who had come to study abroad from Japan.
Three years later, he immigrated to Hitogashima Island, married Kotoko,
and opened Restaurant Kofune.
Kotoko never fully recovered after giving birth to their second daughter Mio and passed away,
but he lives a happy life surrounded by his biological daughters Ushio and Mio, and his foster son Shinpei.
Ushio Kofune

Born 2000 (17), blood type A. Prefectural high school senior on mainland. French father Alain, Japanese mother Kotoko. Blonde hair from father; strong-willed, teased in elementary school for her look. Bakes cookies/chiffon cake. Hates supernatural phenomena. Looked forward to Summer Festival but died July 21 in marine accident. [Summarized - see notes]
Mio Kofune

Born October 20, 2002 (age 15). Blood type A. Younger sibling of Ushio Kofune; takes after mother with black hair, sun-tanned like her youth. High school freshman at same prefectural high as Ushio. Excellent athlete; won swim medals. Loves food, poor cook. Considers Ushio the most beautiful, sees herself as her opposite. Shinpei notes she mirrors Alain's traits in affection and competitiveness. [Summarized - see notes]
Kaori Koyuba ①

Born November 2, 2000. 17 years old. Blood type A.
A former classmate during their days at the Hitogashima Island Elementary and Middle School.
She currently lives in Tokyo, but lived on Hitogashima Island until the 5th grade.
She was shy and withdrawn, and often spent her time reading books in the library.
She loves a character named "Egg Gentleman Eas-ta~kun."
Kaori Koyuba ②

One of the top idols of the present day.
Boasting high vocal and acting abilities, she is widely loved across generations
for her polite and approachable personality, and the occasional glimpses of an airhead.
At the 2017 Red and White Song Battle,
the group she belongs to performed as the closing act. She loves her hometown of Hitogashima Island, and never misses out on her hometown tax donations each year.
Koyuba Channel

The official channel opened by Kaori Koyuba on a video streaming site.
Aside from the latest info on new songs and live concerts,
it is also utilized for interacting with her fans.
Perhaps owing to Kaori's personality and her attitude towards cherishing her fans, her live streams
see virtually no troll comments.
Broken Brakes
The Night of the Tragedy

July 24th, just past 8:30 PM.
The Hitogashima Shrine live concert venue, bustling with excitement from Kaori's appearance,
turned into a hellscape.
A four-armed monster that suddenly appeared
lead an army of Shadows to slaughter the audience one after another.
Shinpei desperately fought back, but was helpless and killed. Is there truly no way to stop that tragedy?
Shizuka Shiomi

The woman living next door to Koba Mart.
She has known Asako Kobayakawa, the owner's wife, for a long time since they were children.
She is talkative and can speak intimately with anyone.
Her neighborhood gossip network surpasses the internet,
making her the most well-informed person regarding the island's human relations.
Project Results Report

Summary of tourism results on Hitogashima Island reveals declining annual tourist numbers, with individual travelers outpacing tour groups. Low youth awareness identified as a key challenge. Proposed improvements include enhancing outreach, transportation access, lodging options, anime/movies promotion, and subsidy applications. [Summarized - see notes]
Shide

Haine's guardian. A massive four-armed monster.
Nothing else is known about him yet besides the name Shide.
Stain Left on the Ground

With just a touch from Shadow Totsumura, Totsumura's corpse vanished without a trace,
leaving nothing but a stain on the ground where it had been.
Incendiary Bomb

A type of bomb designed to start fires.
Filled with oil, they burn the entire surrounding area upon impact.
They were used during the war to burn down the wooden houses that were common in Japan at the time.
Corrosive Sublimate

Mercuric chloride. A deadly poison.
Its handling is strictly restricted by the Poisonous and Deleterious Substances Control Law,
but it used to be employed for disinfection and sterilization.
Direct contact alone can cause dermatitis or nervous system abnormalities,
and it is so highly toxic that drinking even a single drop leads to death.
Shinja Marsh

A pond in Hitogashima Shrine's sacred forest, part of a wetland designated as a prefectural natural monument. Local lore says En no Gyoja sealed a giant serpent here after it tormented Awaji Island and mainland. En no Ozunu cornered it with a divine sword, sparing its life upon promise of good behavior. [Summarized - see notes]
Shinpei's Parents ①

His father is Toru, and his mother is Akemi. Both are underwater archaeologists. An underwater archaeologist is an expert equipped with the skills to survey underwater, and their targets range widely from sunken ships to submerged ruins. Neither was a native of Hitogashima Island, but they loved the island, overflowing with rich nature and history, and lived happy days there. However, 10 years ago in 2008, their boat sank during an accident at work, and they never returned.
Shinpei's Parents ②

A secret medical record was hidden in the Hishigata Clinic. The diagnoses were all listed as Capgras syndrome, but it was actually a registry of those who became food for Haine. The names of Toru and Akemi Ajiro, who supposedly died in a boating accident, were also there. After discovering the Hiruko Cave during a survey in the waters near Hitogashima Island, the two tried to announce it, but were killed by Shide to keep them quiet.
Reason Not to Kill Shinpei

July 24th. Shadow Shinpei, who appeared on the night of the festival, read Shinpei's memories and realized his looping ability. The Shadows seemingly have a grand objective, and Shadow Mio attempts to kill Shinpei, seeing him as an obstacle, but is stopped by Shadow Shinpei. Shadow Shinpei said: "If we let Shinpei die, we might just return to the 22nd again. Therefore, he must not be killed until everything is finished."
The Person I Love

Mio mustered her courage and confessed her feelings,
but Shinpei says the one he loves is Ushio.
Even though she already knew,
hearing it said clearly was a huge shock.
But still, Mio is Shinpei's precious little sister.
Even if they can't be as close as lovers, they won't drift any further apart than they are now.
Mio feels it was better to have bravely spoken up than to live carrying around those clouded feelings.
Snack Miyako

Central-town snack bar near Hitogashima Port. Daytime café popular with island kids. Open 11 AM–6 PM; nightly sessions 7 PM–midnight. Frequented by officer Totsumura, earning it "Miyako Police Box" moniker. Staff secretly dub him "T-800" for his 800-yen liquor orders. [Summarized - see notes]
Champion of Justice

Totsumura's father was a revered police officer who embodied justice. Young Totsumura idolized him as a superhero, inspiring his own career choice. However, Hitogashima Island's peaceful duties clashed with his grand expectations. His laziness led to repeated exam failures, stalling advancement. Yet his inner resolve for justice remains unshaken. [Summarized - see notes]
Time Capsule

A time capsule buried by everyone at school about 7 years ago,
when Kaori moved away.
Not just Shinpei and Kaori, but students from other grades also participated.
Various things were stuffed inside, including toys, notebooks, and letters.
Mt. Takanosu

The mountainous area in the western part of the island.
Wartime historical sites remain here, such as the ruins of the first and third gun batteries, and the searchlight post.
It boasts the highest elevation on the island.
On the west coast lies the Hitogashima Island Lighthouse and the ruins of the second gun battery.
Tokiko's a Shadow!?

July 22nd, just before 8:00 AM. Having shared his memories with his comrades through pseudo-looping, Shinpei and the others gathered at the school to intercept Haine and her forces. Ushio tries to stop Shinpei as he goes to greet Tokiko, who arrived late. However, she didn't make it in the nick of time, and Shinpei was shot to death by Tokiko. Tokiko was a Shadow. Unable to process it, Shinpei's consciousness faded.
Tokiko's Anger

July 22nd, 8:00 AM. Haine will betray the Hishigata family— Even conveying this truth, Tokiko turns a deaf ear. In the fierce battle, Sou takes a fatal wound while protecting Mio. Though shaken by having put Mio and Sou in danger, Tokiko deals the finishing blow to Shinpei with a ghastly expression. Was it out of desperation, or was it to redo everything? Perhaps her anger was directed at herself.
Unsolvable Code

This refers to "Tamam Shud," the cipher that appears in Ryunosuke Nagumo's work "Drift." It originates from a code held by an unidentified corpse discovered in Australia in the 1940s. It contains a string of incomprehensible alphabet letters, and what it represents remains unsolved to this day. In the story of "Drift," it appears in a way that allows for meta-level deduction.
Tokoyo

Haine's homeland, far beyond the distant sea.
A mysterious world that living creatures of this realm cannot reach.
The flow of time is stagnant there, making it an eternal, unchanging world.
doppelgänger

An entity that looks exactly like oneself.
They have been spoken of since ancient times in legends and myths worldwide,
and superstitions claiming that one who sees their doppelgänger will die are also well-known.
Tetsu Totsumura

A police officer working at the island's only police box, and a self-proclaimed "Champion of Justice."
34 years old. A relative of the Kofune family. His trademarks are his Western hat, sunglasses,
and stubble.
Browsing erotic websites is his daily routine. He seems like a womanizer, but he's actually single-minded,
and currently harbors feelings for a woman at a certain snack bar.
Torashima (Tiger Island)

A small island located to the northeast of Hitogashima Island.
Its western side features tombolo topography,
allowing one to walk across from Hitogashima Island only during low tide.
Though not large, it boasts war ruins, remains of a stone quarry, and a cave along the coast previously used as an ascetic training ground.
Ryunosuke Nagumo

A highly mysterious woman who can fight on more than equal footing against the Shadows with superhuman reflexes.
She appeared to save Shinpei.
She shares a name with the sci-fi author Shinpei is a huge fan of,
and her trademarks are a ponytail and glasses.
She looks exactly like the woman he met on the ferry, but it seems this is their first meeting.
Mysterious Shadow

A mysterious Shadow that appeared on the night of the festival.
It possesses a massive frame and four arms.
Its true identity and goals are unknown.
Could it be the boss of the Shadows?
Mysterious Handprint

The mark where Shiori's Shadow grabbed Shinpei's arm,
seen within the memories of the 21st that Ushio showed him.
It remains in the shape of a handprint and can never be erased.
It doesn't hurt, but its exact effects are still unknown.
Narezushi

A traditional local dish passed down from ancient times.
It is said to be the roots of modern sushi,
but it generates its sourness through fermentation rather than using vinegar.
In Wakayama, mackerel and saury are famous.
It is said to boast a history of 1,000 years.
2 Seconds Ahead

Ryunosuke has the ability to see the future. When Ryunosuke's data, which burst out of Haine 14 years ago, was printed onto Hizuru's body, an irregular accident caused the coordinates on the time axis to misalign. Ryunosuke was operating Hizuru from a future 2 seconds ahead. By inheriting Ryunosuke's data, Shinpei has also become capable of this 2-second future precognition.
News Flash

Indiscriminate Terrorism in Wakayama City? (24th, 20:21)
[Wakayama Dispatch] Numerous reports have poured in regarding indiscriminate terrorism at a live concert venue in Wakayama City/Hitogashima Island. The Hitogashima Summer Festival is underway at the Hitogashima Shrine, where the incident occurred, but with no contact with the site, the extent of the damage among attendees remains unknown.
Swampman (Novel)

A novel published by Yotobari Publishing on September 15, 2015. Author: Ryunosuke Nagumo. Hizuru Sagimori believes her family has been replaced by duplicates. A girl resembling her appears and kills her. Explores identity through a doppelgänger's murder. [Summarized - see notes]
Swampman (Philosophy)

A thought experiment by Donald Davidson. A lightning strike destroys a man in a swamp; the tree mirrors his form and memories. The replica replaces him, interacting seamlessly. No one detects the change. This entity is termed "Swampman. [Summarized - see notes]
Sayuri Negoro

A nurse working at the Hishigata Clinic. 38 years old. Lives with her husband.
She is skilled at drawing blood, and the islanders praise her for being completely painless.
She practiced classical ballet from kindergarten through middle school,
and prides herself on her flexibility.
Her husband Daisuke is a systems engineer, originally from Hitogashima Island.
Kaoru Nezu

Ginjiro Nezu's wife.
Two months ago, she went to do farm work, and when she returned, she was a Shadow.
She is pinned to the wall with a nail gun he fired on reflex.
Unable to deal the finishing blow to a Shadow that has the appearance and memories of his beloved wife, Nezu agonized in conflict.
Ginjiro Nezu ①

A stubborn, artisan-minded, one-eyed hunter.
He knows the topography, flora, and fauna of Hitogashima Island inside and out.
He seems to know about the Shadows as well.
He is also a master marksman.
Ginjiro Nezu ②

A one-eyed hunter and master marksman.
He knew many truths about the Shadows, beyond just rumors and legends.
He was the collaborator Hizuru Minakata had mentioned as the true identity of the novelist Ryunosuke Nagumo; a trustworthy figure.
Stray Shadow

Not all Shadows act with a specific purpose in mind.
There appear to be stray Shadows wandering aimlessly without a goal.
Compared to normal Shadows, their copying accuracy seems lacking,
but is there a reason for this?
Haine ①

The young girl in the red kimono floating above the island on the night of the festival.
The four-armed Shadow referred to her as "Mother,"
but Ryunosuke Nagumo called her Haine.
Haine ②

The true identity of Hitogashima Island's god, Hiruko-no-Mikoto,
and the mother of the Shadows.
She resides in a cave deep underground.
In contrast to her dignified demeanor, her face is wrapped in bandages and she receives blood transfusions. She recognizes Ushio as a traitor.
Haine ③

Haine and Hizuru were once friends.
However, losing her ego to hunger, Haine ended up eating Ryunosuke.
Agonizing with guilt, Haine's persona burst out along with the stolen right eye,
and she vanished without a trace.
Her right eye has still not been found even 14 years later.
Haine ④

Hiruko's fate began when she washed up as a whale on Omoto Beach, disrupting Haine and Shide's destiny. Though experiencing human love, Haine endured greed-driven suffering. Human-Shadow coexistence proved impossible. After 300 years of hardship, she sought to erase all memories—love, beauty, the encounter itself. [Summarized - see notes]
Hoine ①

Split from Haine's ego 14 years ago; took right eye, fled sea. Drifted for 14 years, mimicking marine life until drawn back by Haine's Summer Festival signal. Copied Ushio during cleanup drive, now resides within her. [Summarized - see notes]
Hoine ②

Real name Hoine Karikiri.
A fisherman's daughter who lived on Hitogashima Island 300 years ago.
She was copied and eaten by a whale (Hiruko) that washed ashore on the beach.
Afterward, Hiruko came to be worshiped as a god,
and was joined with Shidehiko Hishigata. From then on, Shidehiko assumed the Karikiri surname.
Haine's Handprint

Haine possessed the ability to loop freely.
However, because the world chosen by the observer Shinpei is deemed official history,
she struggled to pinpoint the exact world Shinpei chose from among countless parallel worlds.
But things are different now.
The handprint left on Shinpei's arm serves as an indelible mark, and relying on it,
she can immediately find the world Shinpei travels to.
Hacking

Based on her experience of nearly having her mind tampered with by Haine in the Hiruko Cave,
Ushio figured that if she traced Haine's invasion route backward,
she might be able to erase Haine.
Using that sensation as a hint, she learned to sever the connection between a Shadow and Haine
by tampering with the inside of the Shadow's head. By using this, she can free a Shadow from Haine's control.
Shun Hamaji

The son of the souvenir shop "Hamaji Store." A fifth-grade elementary school student.
A mischievous and rough personality. He hates shiitake mushrooms.
He frequently plays with his sister Akari (5), his schoolmate Shiori Kobayakawa,
and Teru Todai. For what it's worth, Teru's parents run the cafe "Todai."
Panta-sensei

The mascot character for the personal blog "Pantaro Yurahama's Ruins Explorations." The admin visited ruins in various regions and posted photos and reports, always carrying around a stuffed animal they named Panta-sensei. In 2008, an article about the old ward of the Hishigata Clinic was published. It seems they would revisit ruins they took a liking to, but updates suddenly ceased at a certain point. The blog remains abandoned even years later.
Hishigata Clinic

The sole medical institution on Hitogashima Island. Located at the foot of the central mountains. Its history is extremely long, tracing its roots all the way back to the Edo period. Remote islands often face issues with a shortage of doctors and equipment, but Hishigata Clinic performs everything from internal medicine to surgery, supporting the lives of the residents. The clinic director, Seidou Hishigata, commands deep trust from the islanders.
Hishigata Clinic Old Ward

The ward used by Hishigata Clinic until around 1960.
It is no longer in use today,
but half a century has passed without it being demolished.
It sits on land owned by the Hishigata family on Mt. Takanosu.
The decaying ruins have made a name for themselves outside the prefecture as a haunted spot.
Hishigata Family Album

An old album belonging to the Hishigata family.
It is so old that even Sou cannot find any familiar faces in it.
Since all the photos inside are in black and white,
it is believed to be from over half a century ago.
Shidehiko Hishigata

The founder and first director of the Hishigata Clinic.
The Hishigata family has overseen Hitogashima Island's rituals since ancient times,
but in the mid-Edo period, Shidehiko's father began medical practices, forming the origins of the Hishigata Clinic.
Later, a branch of the Hishigata family split off to become the present-day Karikiri family.
Seidou Hishigata ①

The director of the Hishigata Clinic, and Sou and Tokiko's father.
He single-handedly shoulders the medical care of Hitogashima Island,
and was present for the police autopsy when Ushio died in a marine accident.
He holds a very high level of trust from the islanders.
Seidou Hishigata ②

The director of the Hishigata Clinic, and the biological father of Sou and Tokiko.
The Hishigata Clinic collaborated with Haine, and his wife Chitose was a Shadow.
Haine's goal is to return to her homeland.
In that country, which only Shadows can reach, time does not flow.
Seidou was cooperating in order to take his ailing wife and children there
so they could live eternally.
Sou Hishigata

Born November 20, 2000 (17). Blood type O. Son of Hishigata Clinic on Hitogashima Island. Attends Same High School as Ushio; studies for med school. Shinpei's lifelong best friend since transfer student days. Befriended Shinpei after initiating chats. Self-conscious around Mio, whom he likes for her kindness. Struggles with father-son dynamics. [Summarized - see notes]
Chitose Hishigata ①

Sou and Tokiko Hishigata's mother.
Married to Seidou, the director of Hishigata Clinic.
She suffers from a heart condition and lives using a wheelchair.
A donor was found five years ago, but she did not fully recover,
and continues to battle the illness today.
She possesses an extremely kind, family-oriented personality. Sou, Tokiko, and Seidou all cherish Chitose dearly.
Chitose Hishigata ②

Sou and Tokiko's mother.
Chitose spent a long time battling illness.
Sou believed her recovery progressed because a donor was found.
But in reality, she was able to exist because Seidou asked Haine
to turn her into a Shadow.
Tokiko Hishigata ①

Born June 25, 2002. 16 years old, Blood type O.
Sou's younger sister. Childhood friend and best friend to Mio Kofune.
Currently a high school freshman attending a prefectural high school on the mainland.
Highly intelligent with top grades, but physically weak and poor at sports.
She holds a particular disdain for marathons.
She knows how to put on a kimono by herself.
Tokiko Hishigata ②

Sou Hishigata's younger sister, and Mio Kofune's best friend.
According to Ushio,
Tokiko was conversing with the four-armed Shadow during the tragedy on the 24th.
Judging from the conversation, the four-armed Shadow seemed to break a promise with Tokiko,
though the truth of the matter remains uncertain.
Tokiko Hishigata ③

The person they met in the Hiruko Cave was Sou's younger sister, Tokiko.
The real Tokiko, not a Shadow.
The giant stray Shadow she subjugated captured Ushio.
It seems she is trying to hand Ushio over to Hiruko-sama,
but why is she siding with the Shadows?
Hizuru's Deductive Reasoning

Despite being in a state where she couldn't communicate smoothly,
Hizuru deduced Shinpei and the others' positions and circumstances.
It seems her outstanding deductive reasoning
had already been cultivated since her middle school days.
Save Hizuru

July 23rd, just before 11:00 AM. Due to Shadow Shinpei's machinations, Hizuru and the others were split into two groups. After saving Mio and Sou, Shinpei rushed to Torashima (Tiger Island) to help Hizuru. But it was already too late; Hizuru was on the verge of drawing her last breath. If only he had arrived faster, he would have made it in time. Believing this, Shinpei places his bets on the next loop.
Hitogashima Island

Island in Kitan Strait; part of Wakayama City, Wakayama Prefecture. 5.3 km² area, ~700 residents. Primary industries: tourism, fishing; local specialties include mugwort mochi and salted squid guts. Hiruko legend and Meiji-era gun battery ruins are key attractions. Residents use Hitogashima Steamship Company ferries for mainland commutes. [Summarized - see notes]
Hitogashima Island Port

Located on Hitogashima Island's northern side, it serves as the sole ferry terminal linking mainland. Used by tourists and locals for travel, shopping, work/school commutes. Mountains dominate most of island; flat land surrounds port & southern Omoto Beach, where residential zones form concentric rings. [Summarized - see notes]
Hitogashima Elementary & Middle School

The island's only school, attended by the elementary and middle schoolers of Hitogashima Island. The facilities surround the schoolyard with the main gate to the east, the school building to the north, the gymnasium to the west, and the pool situated to the south. The path alongside the pool leads to the islanders' private beach. Due to the size of the grounds and the sturdiness of the buildings, it is designated as the island's evacuation shelter.
Hitogashima Island Lighthouse

A lighthouse standing on the southwest side of Hitogashima Island.
It was established in the early Meiji era based on the Osaka Treaty,
signed to ensure the safe navigation of foreign ships.
It stands 12 meters tall, and its light can reach up to 38 kilometers away.
Owing to its high historical value, it remains a popular tourist spot today.
Hitogashima Shrine

Enshrined on a hill overlooking the Hitogashima Island Port at 1-chome Hitomachi, Wakayama City.
The sacred forest continues into the pristine woods in the eastern part of Hitogashima Island.
Erected in 1732 (Kyoho 17). At the time, it was written as "Hito Shrine."
Generations of the Karikiri family have served as the chief priests.
It is famous for the "Umi-okuri" ritual held every year in summer and winter.
Hitogashima Summer Festival

One of Hitogashima Island's largest summer events at the Hitogashima Shrine. Held July 24, 2018, with a portable shrine procession, fireworks, and Umi-okuri. The shrine travels to Omoto Beach, collects trash, returns, and burns it. A unique tradition drawing hundreds of tourists annually. [Summarized - see notes]
Kanae Hitobuchi

A teacher at Hitogashima Elementary & Middle School. Nicknamed "Bucchi." 58 years old. Taught most local residents for decades. Gentle, straightforward mentor beloved by students. Husband Nobuo works in postal service; sons Kenichi and Yuji serve at town hall. Cheerful household with all family members smiling. [Summarized - see notes]
Hiruko-sama

The nickname for the Hitogashima Shrine situated in the northern part of the island.
It derives from Hiruko-sama, the god of bountiful fishing enshrined at Hitogashima Shrine.
Various legends surrounding Hiruko-sama are passed down on Hitogashima Island.
Before the war, when Shadow Sickness was prevalent,
it is said that those who fell ill were purified by Hiruko-sama.
Statue of Hiruko

A wooden carved statue placed in the operating room of the Hishigata Clinic Old Ward. It holds a fish under its left arm. According to the Kojiki, Hiruko is recognized as the first god born between Izanagi and Izanami during the creation of the land, but was deemed "an imperfect child" and cast away into the sea immediately after birth. Since the name is sometimes written as "Water Leech Child," some theories interpret this to mean its limbs were deformed like a leech.
Hiruko Legend ①

In 1732 (Kyoho 17), Onogoro Island faced starvation during the Great Kyoho Famine. A whale washed ashore, revered by islanders as a castaway god's gift. When a fisherman's daughter approached, the corpse blazed brightly, transforming into her exact replica before devouring her. [Summarized - see notes]
Hiruko Legend ②

Terrified of a curse, islanders enshrined the being in daughter's form as Hiruko-sama, building an underground chamber where he resided—thus creating the Hiruko Cave. His first act post-transformation was consuming human flesh, demanding sacrifices thereafter but granting bountiful catches. Onogoro Island later renamed itself Hitogashima (People Island), evolving into its current spelling. [Summarized - see notes]
Hiruko Cave

A vast subterranean space spreads out beneath Hitogashima Island.
Hitogashima Island's guardian deity, Hiruko-sama, is said to have settled there after washing ashore on the island, earning it the name Hiruko Cave.
It is said there used to be an entrance in the western part of the island that appeared only at low tide,
but it was allegedly sealed off when the military developed the port during the Sino-Japanese War.
Temporary Ferry Schedule
developer_comments: フェリーの臨時ダイヤ

target:
The final regular ferry for Hitogashima Steamship is the 18:30 departure from Hitogashima Island,
but extra ferries run on the day of the Summer Festival when many tourists visit.

Departs Kada Port  Departs Hitogashima
19:00       19:30
20:00       20:30
21:00       21:30
22:00       22:30
developer_comments: フェリーの臨時ダイヤ

target: Bird's-Eye View

Shinpei Ajiro's habit.
Having another version of himself observe him from a high vantage point,
to view things with a broad perspective.
By taking a bird's-eye view to oversee his own position and the surrounding situation,
Shinpei maintains cool-headed thinking at all times, notices things that are hard to catch,
and has lived his life without getting depressed even when making mistakes.
Plan B

The operation to eliminate the Shadows that replaced the Kobayakawa family.
Initially, Plan A envisioned Hizuru storming in and eradicating them left and right,
but it was changed to Plan B because the daughter, Shiori, slipped away.
The new plan positions Shinpei and Nezu on standby
along Shiori's escape route, where they will intercept her as she flees.
Tadashi Miura

A detective attached to the Wakayama North Police Station's Community Safety Division. Rank: Sergeant.
He arrived on the island to investigate the Kobayakawa family's disappearance.
He is racking his brain over the many suspicious elements, such as the entire family vanishing and no trace of them leaving the island.
Mio Fell in the Sea

July 22nd, before noon.
As Mio rode her bicycle down the hill to welcome Shinpei,
she carried too much momentum and plummeted into the sea.
There was a slight accident where Shinpei caught a glimpse of her striped underwear,
but fortunately, she was uninjured.
Mio's Feelings

Mio mustered her courage and confessed her feelings. But Shinpei did not reciprocate. To him, Mio is a precious younger sister, and he could not view her as a romantic interest. Even though she knew this, Mio's chest tightened with a painful ache. But it is also true she feels much lighter than when she kept her feelings bottled up inside; perhaps this was a rite of passage for her to move forward.
Hizuru Minakata ①

The true identity of the masked author "Ryunosuke Nagumo"; her real name is Hizuru Minakata.
A Hitogashima Island native of unknown age.
A tall beauty who wears glasses and boasts a G-cup chest.
She seems to be using a gun to fight the Shadows, but her reasons and goals are unknown.
Hizuru Minakata ②

A woman native to Hitogashima Island. Born July 7, 1989. 14 years ago, middle-schooler Hizuru and Haine were close friends. But one day, driven to madness by hunger, Haine devoured and killed Hizuru's younger brother, Ryunosuke. Agonizing with guilt over killing her best friend's brother, Haine's persona burst out along with Ryunosuke's data. Ryunosuke's data entered Hizuru, and ever since, the Shadow Ryunosuke has resided within her.
Ryunosuke Minakata ①

Ryunosuke Minakata ① Hizuru's twin brother found dead on Mt. Takanosu 14 years ago. Police ruled wild dogs; Hizuru claimed murder due to strangulation marks among bite wounds. Autopsy cited hemorrhagic shock, closing the case. Nezu used his name during Koba Mart and at a festival, motive unknown. [Summarized - see notes]
Ryunosuke Minakata ②

Hizuru Minakata's twin brother. A middle-schooler born on July 7, 1989.
He lives together with his father Tora and mother Tsukiko as a family of four.
Unlike the cool-headed Hizuru, he possesses a sociable and friendly personality.
He is also close friends with Shiori Kobayakawa's mother, Asako (maiden name Isogane).
Ryunosuke Minakata ③

It seemed Ryunosuke's life was lost in the tragedy 14 years ago. However, because Ryunosuke's data, which Haine scanned, entered Hizuru, Ryunosuke's mind continues to live on within Hizuru. When Hizuru ties her hair up, their personalities can swap, and while Ryunosuke controls the body, he can move beyond human limits utilizing the power of a Shadow. On Hizuru's palm, a burn-like mark remains from where Ryunosuke was printed.
Corpse Armor

Created by Nayuta Karikiri's second generation for wartime use, it remains in continuous development. Formed from deteriorated Shadowness. Retains no data; Shide controls via consciousness transfer. Can reshape or extend but lacks flatting/copying capabilities. Interior acts as subspace, blocking external harm. Extremely powerful yet hides a deeper mystery. [Summarized - see notes]
Mesopotamian Civilization

Around 3000 BC, the world's oldest urban civilization arose in Mesopotamia's Tigris-Euphrates basin. Frequent floods inspired theories linking its history to Noah's Ark. Sou Hishigata proposed "Mesopotamian Civilization" as a password for Shadows vs humans, chosen via word association from "Mountain/River. [Summarized - see notes]
Eye Color

The color of Shinpei Ajiro's right eye had changed from before.
Shinpei himself has no idea when or why it changed.
Immunity

Even if you contract Shadow Sickness, killing your born Shadow grants permanent immunity. This state, likened to measles, prevents future copies. Immunity reduces life-targeting risks from Shadows and eliminates memory theft by duplicates. [Summarized - see notes]
Yamabiko

Shide's armor is equipped with a special ability called "Yamabiko,"
which can redirect any energy it receives straight back at the opponent.
Restaurant Kofune

Established in 1994. The only Western-style restaurant on Hitogashima Island.
While every islander has surely visited at least once,
it also enjoys a very good reputation among tourists.
It is especially bustling during lunchtime;
the A-Lunch Pork Cutlet, B-Lunch Fried White Fish,
and C-Lunch Neapolitan boast unforgettable flavors once you've tasted them.
Yoshikawa Lines

Marks left on the victim's neck.
They indicate that the victim resisted,
making the possibility of suicide extremely low.
Loop Rules

When Shinpei dies, time rewinds and he can try again.
However, with each successive loop,
the amount of time he can rewind gradually grows shorter,
and any events that occurred prior to the time he rewound to become
"locked-in" events that can never be overturned.
It seems loops are not omnipotent.
Concerns Regarding the Loop

Shadows' July 24th festival massacre begins. Shinpei considers using time loops to prevent it, but faces uncertainty about activation conditions—dying may not trigger a loop or send him forward. His focus remains on surviving to protect Mio and honor Ushio's promise. [Summarized - see notes]
Ros and Guildenstern

The stray Shadows Haine assigned to guard Tokiko. Their official names are Rosencrantz and Guildenstern. Tokiko named them after characters from Shakespeare's Hamlet. Ros has an absentminded personality, while Guil, whose right hand is injured, is short-tempered. Shadows entirely specialized for combat like them are called Hitodenashi (Inhuman); by sacrificing their human forms, their combat prowess improves in exchange for lower intelligence.
1st END

July 23rd, 3:00 PM. Shinpei and Mio spot Shiori at Hitogashima Shrine, chase her into the sacred forest. They reunite with a wounded woman near the 4th gun battery ruins. She tries to warn Shinpei before being shot by an unseen attacker. Turning back, Shinpei sees Mio holding a handgun—the school-uniformed Mio shoots the other Mio, then aims at Shinpei. [Summarized - see notes]
2nd END

July 22nd, 9:00 AM. Shinpei ponders July 22nd at 9 AM. Totsumura patrols; Mio stands in uniform instead of bathing. She stabs him, spawns a Totsumura clone that replaces the real one. Shinpei witnesses and is murdered again by Mio. [Summarized - see notes]
3rd END

July 24th, 9 PM: Summer Festival climax triggers Umi-okuri-induced Shadow outbreak. Shinpei saved by Ryunosuke Nagumo (shotgun), who confronts four-armed Shadow. Shadows' leader "Mother" emerges; Shinpei begs to be killed to stop the massacre. [Summarized - see notes]
4th END

Jul 23: Joining Shadow Ushio, Shinpei's team travels to Hiruko Cave, meeting Haine—the shadow ringleader posing as Shiori for mass slaughter. They confront the four-armed Shadow blocking their path, but Shinpei loops by consuming Old Ward poison after Shadows try stopping his suicide. [Summarized - see notes]
5th END

July 22nd, before 7:00 AM. Shinpei's team gains Hizuru/Nezu's trust via memory-sharing. They plot to destroy Haine at Koba Mart but fail; Shadows kill them. Haine tracks Shinpei through his brand; he survives Ushio's throat-cutting loop. [Summarized - see notes]
6th END

July 22nd, 8:00 AM.
All-out warfare is the only way to counter Haine and Shide.
Shinpei decides to share Ushio's memories with his comrades,
uniting their strength to intercept Haine.
However, Shinpei's consciousness is suddenly cut short,
and he is thrust back into the past once again.
7th END

July 23rd, before 2:00 AM.
Shide's true identity was Masahito Karikiri.
Storming the Hitogashima Shrine, Shinpei demands to know why he killed his parents.
He succeeds in defeating Karikiri, who does nothing but smile fearlessly,
but Ushio is suddenly killed by another Karikiri appearing from behind.
8th END

July 23rd, 11:00 AM. Haine's disguise as Shadow Shinpei splits the group; Mio heads SW fields, Hizuru to Torashima (Tiger Island). Reuniting with Shadow Mio, Shinpei rushes to Mio's field but finds them in Hiruko Cave for Mother. Tokiko reports uphill battle on Torashima. Shinpei loops, vowing rescue before taking his life. [Summarized - see notes]
9th END

July 24th, before 6:00 AM.
The Ushio he had been traveling with all this time was the Ushio from the 3rd loop.
By having the 9th loop's Ushio read the memories she left behind,
Shinpei and his allies magnificently resurrected Ushio.
In order to outwit Haine and the others,
Shinpei opted to loop from an advantageous position—what could his true intentions be?
route: Kaori Koyuba

Shinpei reunites with a young girl at the Hitogashima Island port. It's Kaori Koyuba, his classmate from elementary school. She says she was invited to perform in a live concert event at the Hitogashima Summer Festival. Shinpei is surprised that this girl, who gave off such a quiet and shy impression, has become a popular idol. Largely due to her appearance, this year's Summer Festival records unprecedented attendance, but behind the scenes, a terrifying plan is being devised.
route: Tetsu Totsumura

Feeling a sense of crisis regarding the existence of the Shadows, Shinpei and Mio rely on Totsumura to enlist the help of the police. Their feelings get through to a half-doubting Totsumura, who begins to burn with his mission as a Hero of Justice. However, with a detective from the mainland going missing immediately after arriving on the island, the demonic hands of the Shadows are steadily drawing closer. Is there truly a way to defeat the Shadows?
route: Mio Kofune

On the night of the Hitogashima Summer Festival, the shrine grounds are thrown into a panic by the sudden appearance of the Shadows. Shinpei grabs Mio's hand and flees. To keep his promise to Ushio, he cannot leave Mio all alone. He will protect her, even at the cost of his own life. Having resolved this, Shinpei regroups with Alain and considers escaping from the island, but is completely unaware that they have walked right into the enemy's trap.
route: Hizuru Minakata

Transported into the memories of Hizuru's middle school days, Shinpei and Ushio
learn that Hizuru and Haine used to be close friends.
Perhaps they can change history.
Thinking this, Shinpei and Ushio succeed in interfering with past events.
However, things don't go entirely as planned,
and a tribulation different from the original history awaits them.
route: Tokiko Hishigata

Tokiko finds herself agonizing between her beloved family and Haine on one side,
and her best friends like Mio on the other.
She just wants to live a peaceful, happy life,
but caught in relationships that cannot coexist, unable to consult anyone,
only her sins and suffering continue to swell.
At last, the time comes for her to make a decision.
Was her choice the right one? Nobody knows.
route: Ushio Kofune

Shinpei returns to Hitogashima Island to attend Ushio's funeral. He and Ushio had an argument over his move to Tokyo, and hadn't made up since. Shinpei fiercely regrets that he postponed it when he had countless opportunities to make amends. Strange things begin happening around him. Eerie Shadows strolling the island, and the inexplicable phenomenon of returning to the past every time he is killed. And then, Shinpei reunites with the supposedly dead Ushio.
epilogue: Kaori Koyuba

Kaori shares memories of Hitogashima Island at a talk show. She hunts for material with Shinpei and others but struggles. Ushio recalls the time capsule buried when Kaori left school. It offers insight into her past, sparking hope to awaken lost memories. The group searches using limited clues, though Kaori grows dispirited. Inside lies a secret she refuses to reveal. [Summarized - see notes]
epilogue: Mio Kofune

Shinpei and Ushio reunite for the first time in two years. Even though they aren't officially dating, seeing them mutually yearn for each other leaves a cloud in Mio's heart. Shinpei treats her gently without noticing her feelings. What does she want to do? What should she do? To get rid of this cloud and take even a small step forward, Mio resolves to confess her feelings.
epilogue: Hizuru Minakata

Stuck on ideas for her new novel, Hizuru returns to Hitogashima Island seeking a way out of her slump. She intended to quietly brainstorm on her own, but ends up having to look after her niece, Hoine. Though initially bewildered, acting alongside Hoine makes Hizuru realize she is viewing the island from a different perspective than usual. Gathering the fragments of sparkling ideas left in their footprints, Hizuru weaves a new tale.
epilogue: Ushio Kofune

Shinpei and Ushio reunite for the first time in two years. Things had been awkward between them since Shinpei moved to Tokyo, but driven by terribly overwhelming affection and the fear of never seeing each other again, they instinctively embrace. Even though they continue to drift past each other afterward, they remain connected in their hearts, reaffirming that they are absolutely indispensable to one another.
